text: This is Oplit Copilot. A real-time decision center where factory leaders run their entire production network through financial metrics.
Drilling into the Gien plant, the agents have detected critical alerts and recommend actions the manager can take or delegate to the AI.
The AI agents work autonomously. They rerouted batch orders, triggered a forward purchase before a price increase, and flagged a capacity risk before it became critical.
The analytics layer turns raw operational data into financial clarity. Run rates, margins, inventory levels, all tracked across sites.
Each plant has its own management view. The plant director sees exactly where the gaps are and which product references need attention.

Here is what makes this possible. Oplit produces a detailed, realistic production schedule spanning weeks into the future. Every machine, every order, planned to the minute after comparing millions of scenarios.

Because the schedule is so detailed, every operator on the floor knows exactly what to produce, on which machine, at any time. The instructions are always the best possible trade-off between service levels, inventory, and costs.

The AI module ties it all together. A dashboard analyst to easily build reports on top of the factory Knowledge Models, a recommendation engine to have strategic discussions with the AI, and specialized agents like Claire who orchestrates supply chain trade-offs across all sites.

Finally, the planning rules engine. Managers define business rules. The AI analyzes impact, simulates changes, and routes them through a governed approval workflow. From human intent to validated deployment. This is the future: factories piloted by AI, with humans in command.
